steps.title: Identify the central oversight
steps.content: The passage contrasts what scholars recognize (Zelda's influence on F. Scott) with what many overlook: that Zelda was a writer in her own right. The evidence—her novel Save Me the Waltz and short stories—confirms this overlooking as a genuine gap in understanding.

steps.title: Determine the consequence of that gap
steps.content: Viewing Zelda only as inspiration for F. Scott means risking misrepresentation of her full range of contributions to literature. A shifts focus to F. Scott's other motivations, C to their mutual views, and D to reading style. That confirms B is correct.
